parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf01329-request`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Classify with AI

### Classify with AI

LLM classification into: Action Needed, Reference, Ignore (+fallback). Output port order matches this list.

Ports: ①→ Store as Action Needed · ②→ Store as Reference · ③→ Store as Ignore · ④→ Alert Outlook — Other

### OpenRouter Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Classify with AI** as `languageModel`

🔑 OpenRouter API key

### Store as Action Needed

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Next: Reunite Branches

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Store as Reference

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Next: Reunite Branches

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Store as Ignore

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Next: Reunite Branches

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Alert Outlook — Other

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Reunite Branches

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Reunite Branches

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Sort Requests Newest First

### Sort Requests Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Send Summary to Outlook

### Send Summary to Outlook

microsoftOutlook — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

# Digest documents with Google Sheets and Outlook

**Pattern:** AI Summarize & Route · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Digest documents with Google Sheets and Outlook. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual multi-tool agent is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Classify with AI → Store as Action Needed → Reunite Branches → Sort Requests Newest First → Send Summary to Outlook

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai summarize & route without manual effort
- Anyone automating multi-tool agent in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Standardizes the process every time

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; OpenRouter API key.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your multi-tool agent process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
